zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Multon於大約1306年出生在英國波藍德艾格雷蒙特時，她的父親Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont31歲，母親Eleanor de Burgh25歲。她於大約1336年與Walter de Birmingham結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1344年在英國藍卡爾阿爾迪加姆享年39歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Multon于大约1306年出生在英国波藍德艾格雷蒙特时，她的父亲Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont31岁，母亲Eleanor de Burgh25岁。她于大约1336年与Walter de Birmingham结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1344年在英国藍卡爾阿爾迪加姆享年39岁。

nameMeanings.name: Multon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Moulton
meanings: English (mainly Cheshire and Lancashire): habitational name from one or more of the places called Moulton (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire, Suffolk, North Yorkshire), most of which are named with the Old English personal name Mūl(a), the Old Norse personal name Múli, or Old English mūl ‘mule’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Multon was born about 1306, in Egremont, Cumberland, England, her father, Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont, was 31 and her mother, Eleanor de Burgh, was 25. She married Walter de Birmingham about 1336. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1344, in Aldingham, Lancashire, England, at the age of 39.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Multon est née environ 1306, à Egremont, Cumberland, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Eleanor de Burgh, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Walter de Birmingham environ 1336. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1344, à Aldingham, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 39 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Multon um 1306 in Egremont, Cumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont 31 und ihre Mutter Eleanor de Burgh 25. Sie hat um 1336 Walter de Birmingham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1344 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Aldingham, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Multon è nata il circa 1306 nel Egremont, Cumberland, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Eleanor de Burgh, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Walter de Birmingham circa 1336. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1344, nel Aldingham, Lancashire, England, all'età di 39 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Multonが約1306年にイギリスクウンバーランドエグレモントで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremontは31歳，母Eleanor de Burghは25歳であった。約1336年にWalter de Birminghamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1344年にイギリスランカシュアイアアルディンガムで39歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Multon(이)가 대략 1306년 영국 c벌랜드 그렘트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor de Burgh(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1336년 Walter de Birmingham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1344년 영국 랭카실르 알딩함에서 39세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Multon nasceu aproximadamente 1306, em Egremont, Cumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor de Burgh, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Walter de Birmingham aproximadamente 1336. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1344, em Aldingham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 39 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Multon родилась около 1306 г. в местности эгремонт, кумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Eleanor de Burgh, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter de Birmingham около 1336 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1344 г. в местности алдингхам, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 39 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Elizabeth Multon nació aproximadamente 1306, en Egremont, Cumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Eleanor de Burgh, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Walter de Birmingham aproximadamente 1336. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1344, en Aldingham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 39 años.
